# rkit — full reference for LLMs

> General-purpose Go library (`github.com/reitmas32/rkit`, Go 1.25+) with base
> utilities for microservices: structured errors, a generic Result type, an
> error-accumulating context, generic repositories, an event bus, and a
> structured logger with a batched Grafana Loki hook.

Install: `go get github.com/reitmas32/rkit`. Import packages by full path. Every
package has a `doc.go` comment and runnable `Example` functions on pkg.go.dev.
This file expands `llms.txt` with API summaries and copy-pasteable snippets; the
authoritative, version-pinned reference is https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/reitmas32/rkit.

---

## core/kerrors — structured errors

Error type with `Message`, `Code int`, `Metadata map[string]any` and `Cause error`.
`Error()` returns the message; `Unwrap()` returns the cause, so `errors.Is` /
`errors.Unwrap` work.

- `NewKError(message string, code int, metadata map[string]any) *KError`
- `NewKErrorWithCause(message string, code int, metadata map[string]any, cause error) *KError`
- `(*KError).WithCause(cause error) *KError`

```go
err := kerrors.NewKError("user not found", 404, map[string]any{"user_id": "u-123"})
dbErr := errors.New("connection timeout")
wrapped := kerrors.NewKErrorWithCause("failed to save user", 500, nil, dbErr)
errors.Is(wrapped, dbErr) // true
```

## core/result — functional error handling

Generic `Result[T]` inspired by Rust's `Result<T, E>`.

- Constructors: `Ok[T](value T)`, `Err[T](*kerrors.KError)`, `Empty[T]()`, `NewOkResult`, `NewErrResult`.
- Methods: `IsOk() bool`, `IsEmpty() bool`, `Value() T`, `Error() error`, `ToKError() *kerrors.KError`.

```go
func divide(a, b int) result.Result[float64] {
    if b == 0 {
        return result.Err[float64](kerrors.NewKError("division by zero", 400, nil))
    }
    return result.Ok(float64(a) / float64(b))
}

r := divide(10, 2)
if r.IsOk() { fmt.Println(r.Value()) } // 5
```

## core/customctx — error-accumulating context

Implements `context.Context` while collecting structured errors and carrying a logger.

- `New(parent context.Context) *CustomContext`, `NewWithValues(parent, values)`.
- `AddError(*kerrors.KError) *kerrors.KError`, `HasErrors() bool`, `Errors() []WrapError`, `FirstError()`, `LastError()`, `Clear()`.
- `WithLogger(logger.ILogger) *CustomContext`, `Logger() logger.ILogger`, `Context() context.Context`.

```go
cc := customctx.New(context.Background())
cc.AddError(kerrors.NewKError("validation failed", 400, nil))
if cc.HasErrors() {
    for _, e := range cc.Errors() { _ = e }
}
```

## core/logger — logging contract

- `ILogger` interface: `Debug/Info/Warn/Error/Fatal/Panic(message string, args ...any)`.
- `SimpleLogger`: `NewSimpleLogger(level string) *SimpleLogger` (debug|info|warn|error).
- Levels: `ParseLevel`, `IsLoggable`, `IsLoggableLevel`.

```go
log := logger.NewSimpleLogger("info")
log.Info("server started on port %d", 8080)
```

## core/eventbus — event bus contracts

Interfaces only: `Event`, `Message`, `Publisher`, `Consumer`, `EventBus`,
`HandlerFunc func(ctx context.Context, event Event) error`, `Metadata map[string]string`.
Implementations live under `infrastructure/eventbus`.

## core/http — HTTP contracts + typed helpers

Request/response contracts plus generics that (de)serialize JSON:
`PostTyped[Req, Res]`, `PutTyped`, `PatchTyped`, and `TypedResponse[T]` with
`IsSuccess()`, `IsClientError()`, `IsServerError()`. Use with the
`infrastructure/http` client.

## core/types — domain identifiers

`DomainID` encodes a module code and entity code alongside a UUID.

- `NewDomainID(moduleHex, entityHex string) (DomainID, error)`
- `ParseDomainID(s string) (DomainID, error)`

```go
id, err := types.NewDomainID("02", "01")
```

## infrastructure/http — HTTP client

`net/http`-based client with retries, logging and customctx integration.

- `NewClient(Config) *Client`, `DefaultConfig() Config`.
- `Get/Post/Put/Patch/Delete/Head/Options(ctx *customctx.CustomContext, url string, ...)`.
- `NewCustomContextFromGin(ginCtx) *customctx.CustomContext`.

```go
cc := customctx.New(context.Background())
client := infrahttp.NewClient(infrahttp.DefaultConfig())
resp, err := client.Get(cc, "https://example.com/health")
```

## infrastructure/eventbus — implementations

- `inmemory.NewEventBus() *EventBus` — in-process; ideal for tests/dev.
- `rabbit.NewEventBus(config Config, eventFactory func(eventName string) eventbus.Event) (*EventBus, error)` — RabbitMQ, incl. delayed delivery. See `examples/infrastructure/eventbus/rabbit`.

## infrastructure/dtos — gin DTO binding

`GetDTO[K]`, `GetDTOWithResponse[K]`, `GetDTONonDestructive[K]`,
`GetAuthToken`, `GetAuthTokenWithEarlyResponse` — bind/validate request bodies and
extract bearer tokens, integrating with `result` and `customctx`.

## persistence/contracts — storage abstractions

`IEntity` (domain entity) and `IModel` (DB row), plus `EntityToModel`,
`ModelToEntity`, `ToJSON`, `FromJSON`. Repositories are written against these so
domain code stays storage-agnostic.

## persistence/criteria — composable filters

`Filter{Field FilterField, Operator Operator, Value any}` grouped by `Filters`.
Operators: `OperatorEqual`, `OperatorNotEqual`, `OperatorGreaterThan`,
`OperatorGreaterEqual`, `OperatorLessThan`, `OperatorLessEqual`, `OperatorLike`,
`OperatorNotLike`, `OperatorIn`, `OperatorNotIn`.

```go
filters := criteria.NewFilters([]criteria.Filter{
    {Field: "status", Operator: criteria.OperatorEqual, Value: "active"},
    {Field: "age", Operator: criteria.OperatorGreaterThan, Value: 18},
})
```

## persistence/pagination — pages

- `NewPageRequest(page, size int) PageRequest` (page is 0-indexed); `Offset()`, `Limit()`.
- `Pageable` adds `Sort{Field, Direction}` with `SortDirectionASC`/`SortDirectionDESC`.
- `PageResult[T]{Content, TotalElements, TotalPages, Page, Size, IsFirst, IsLast, HasNext, HasPrevious}`; `NewPageResult(content, totalElements, pageRequest)`.
- `CriteriaPageable` combines a `criteria.Criteria` with a `Pageable`.

```go
req := pagination.NewPageRequest(0, 20)
page := pagination.NewPageResult(items, total, req)
```

## persistence/models — base entity

`Entity` (embeddable: ID, timestamps, soft-delete, `ToJSON`) and mutation
notifications: `OnMutationFunc`, `NotifyMutation`.

```go
type UserEntity struct {
    models.Entity
    Email string
}
```

## persistence/inmemory, postgres, mongodb — repositories

Generic repositories over `contracts`, typed by entity and model. Standard ops:
Save, GetByID, UpdateByFields, DeleteByID, criteria-based matching.

- `inmemory.NewInMemoryMapRepository[E, M](onMutation models.OnMutationFunc)`.
- `postgres.PostgresRepository[E, M]{Connection: db}` (GORM).
- `mongodb` repository (go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver/v2).

```go
repo := &postgres.PostgresRepository[UserEntity, UserModel]{Connection: db}
repo.Save(cc, entity)
```

## observability/logger/loguru — structured logger

logrus-based implementation of `core/logger.ILogger`. Console format:
`DATE_TIME | LEVEL | FILE.FUNCTION:LINE | [fields] | MESSAGE`. Optional ANSI colors.

- `NewLogger(fields Fields) *Logger`, `NewLoggerWithConfig(fields, Config{Level, Colorable})`.
- `AddHook(logrus.Hook)`, `SetConfig`, `GetLogrusLogger`.
- `fields`: `HTTPFileds`, `WSFields` (UpdateOne/UpdateAll/ToFields/Format).
- `hooks.NewLokiBufferedHook(url string, batchSize int, baseLabels map[string]string) *LokiBufferedHook` with `Flush() error`. Buffers entries and POSTs to Loki's `/loki/api/v1/push` once batchSize (default 50) is reached; call Flush() for partial batches (e.g. at the end of a request). Failed POSTs keep the buffer.

```go
f := &fields.HTTPFileds{}
f.UpdateOne("method", "GET")
f.UpdateOne("path", "/api/v1/users")

log := loguru.NewLogger(f)

hook := hooks.NewLokiBufferedHook(
    "http://localhost:3100/loki/api/v1/push", 50,
    map[string]string{"app": "my-service", "environment": "production"},
)
log.AddHook(hook)
defer hook.Flush()

log.Info("request received")
```

A mock Loki server for local development is in `mock/loki/`.
